title: aws-ssm put() maps all errors to Unavailable
description: Any error from put_parameter() (including AccessDeniedException, InvalidKeyId) is mapped to SecretError::Unavailable, telling callers to retry. get() correctly distinguishes these as permanent Backend errors. Trigger: call put() on a parameter you lack ssm:PutParameter permission for. Bad behavior: get Unavailable (retry-able) instead of Backend (permanent). The put() error mapper at secretx-aws-ssm/src/lib.rs:158 is a single-arm closure mapping everything to Unavailable — it was not updated when get() got its refined error mapping in the P1-P4 round.
